news.title: Congressional Democrats try to force a vote to end Medicare AI prior authorization pilot
news.summary: Congressional Democrats are mounting a fresh effort to end a Medicare experiment to use AI to approve or deny care.

news.title: STAT+: Pioneering trial for treating genetic disease before birth nears reality
news.summary: A UCSF team has submitted an application to the FDA seeking approval for a small trial of in utero gene therapy for a rare lysosomal storage disorder.

news.title: Surgeon general’s office issues warning on screen time for children
news.summary: A public health advisory calls on parents, schools, and government to limit children's use of screens, saying some patterns of use “can pose real harm to children.”

news.title: STAT+: Florida hospitals win $8 billion in extra Medicaid funds
news.summary: Stricter limits on extra Medicaid funds are coming, stemming from President Trump's tax cut bill.

news.title: STAT+: Scientists track cellular disruptions that lead to type 1 diabetes
news.summary: Growing evidence suggests it takes a sustained viral infection in the right genetic environment to lead to type 1 diabetes.

news.title: Opinion: I saw Ebola as both doctor and patient. I wish people cared more about the Africans I worked alongside
news.summary: “As another Ebola outbreak unfolds, we cannot repeat the mistakes of the past,” writes Krutika Kuppalli.

news.title: STAT+: Biotech execs, academic expert lament impact of FDA turnover on rare disease drug development
news.summary: How the ouster of key officials at the FDA has created anxiety and uncertainty for the developers of rare disease drugs.

news.title: STAT+: Kennedy fires two leaders on preventive services task force
news.summary: Kennedy will have broad latitude to remake the panel, which advises the government on types of preventive care that should be covered by health insurance.

news.title: STAT+: Drugmakers guard IP more tightly amid China competition
news.summary: The FDA's new path, the status of animal testing, and other biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Kennedy names interim surgeon general amid vacancies atop health department
news.summary: Stephanie Haridopolos, a family medicine physician and senior HHS adviser, will take on the surgeon general's duties on an interim basis.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about biotech investors’ mood, a former FDA head’s confidence, and more
news.summary: For Wall Street, the political risk of investing in drug companies has been demoted in comparison to other concerns

news.title: There’s a leadership vacuum at NIH, too
news.summary: USAID cuts have painful outcomes for the Ebola outbreak, states sue over a new professional ed loan rule, and other health news

news.title: After a critique from Rubio, WHO defends work on Ebola response
news.summary: The WHO defended its Ebola response, noting it's national authorities who detect outbreaks. Marco Rubio had said the WHO was "a little late."

news.title: STAT+: Senate Democrats propose adding long-term care benefit to Medicare
news.summary: It's part of Democrats' strategy to focus on health care affordability and access in drawing contrasts with Republicans ahead of the midterms.

news.title: STAT+: NIH behind in filling top roles, with 15 of 27 institutes led by acting directors
news.summary: An NIH leadership vacuum has persisted for months, with 15 of the 27 institutes being led by acting directors.

news.title: Kennedy dismisses leaders of US Preventive Services Task Force
news.summary: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. removed two key leaders of the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force, a key advisory panel that offers guidance on preventive services, according to media reports.

news.title: CMS proposes rule aimed at limiting Medicaid state-directed payments
news.summary: The Trump administration has proposed a "sweeping crackdown" on Medicaid payments that it says have significantly inflated costs in the program.

news.title: WTW: Employers aiming to bulk up AI use for health and benefits
news.summary: Employers are set to significantly increase their use of AI in health benefits, a new survey shows, even as they continue to face barriers to rolling out the tech.

news.title: 'Missed risk': Women's Heart Health Summit explores gaps in research, treatment
news.summary: The event, in its third year, brings together stakeholders across healthcare, including clinicians, researchers, employers, advocates and more, to share learnings and facilitate partnerships to advance women’s health.

news.title: House and Senate Democrats move to overturn CMS’ WISeR AI prior auth pilot
news.summary: The resolution comes after the GAO ruled the model is subject to a vote under the Congressional Review Act.

news.title: Democratic senators share plans for Medicare home care benefit, long-term care reform
news.summary: Access to affordable long-term care, including home care, is the next healthcare pitch from minority party lawmakers ahead of midterms.

news.title: GHO Capital, CBC Group plan to merge, forming $21B healthcare investment firm
news.summary: London-based Global Healthcare Opportunities and Singapore-based CBC Group announced plans to merge, creating what they called the largest healthcare-focused investment manager, with over $21 billion in assets.

news.title: Maven Clinic officially launches DTC platform for GLP-1s, hormone care
news.summary: Maven’s consumer platform now comprises three core offerings: virtual specialty care, GLP-1 care and hormone care.

news.title: The broken pipeline of mental healthcare for LGBTQ teenagers
news.summary: As access to affirming mental healthcare for LGBTQ youth shrinks, more teens are running into barriers to getting care. In this week’s episode of “Podnosis,” reporters from Fierce Healthcare and Uncloseted Media discuss the reporting behind their recent collaboration and the stories they heard from trans teens, their families and experts navigating a strained mental healthcare system.

news.title: Labcorp joins lab results personalization push with ‘MyLabcorp’ AI app
news.summary: The big new thing in medtech for 2026 is AI-powered apps and chatbots designed to help patients navigate their laboratory test results. Now, testing giant Labcorp is getting in on the act.

news.title: Doximity inks partnerships with Aledade, Photon as it ramps up AI spending in 2026
news.summary: The company plans to scale its clinical AI suite, including its ambient notetaking tool Scribe and clinical AI assistant and medical search engine Ask (formerly DoxGPT).

news.title: Coalition of attorneys general sue to block federal student loan caps
news.summary: Twenty-six attorneys general filed a lawsuit Tuesday against the Department of Education over a recently finalized restrictions on federal student loans the litigators, as well as health industry groups, say will hamper the clinical workforce pipeline.

news.title: US taps small San Diego biotech for experimental Ebola treatment as epidemic worsens
news.summary: As an outbreak of Ebola in central Africa continues to worsen, the federal government has tapped a little-known biotech in San Diego to provide doses of an experimental antibody that may be used to treat the deadly virus.

news.title: GHO Capital, CBC Group plan to merge, forming $21B healthcare investment firm
news.summary: London-based Global Healthcare Opportunities and Singapore-based CBC Group announced plans to merge, creating what they called the largest healthcare-focused investment manager, with over $21 billion in assets.

news.title: Qiagen links with chip giant Nvidia to propel AI use in drug discovery
news.summary: Qiagen, a Netherlands-based diagnostics maker, is linking with tech giant Nvidia in a collaboration aimed at boosting the ability of researchers to leverage AI in the drug discovery process.

news.title: Lilly pens $202M deal for preclinical DNA delivery biotech Engage as M&A streak continues
news.summary: San Carlos-based Engage is developing non-viral DNA delivery systems that aim to overcome traditional limitations associated with the modality.

news.title: With $30M launch, Oorja Bio breathes new life into South Korean fibrosis candidate
news.summary: In the sprawling metropolis of Houston, a new biotech has emerged to tackle the biology underlying a lung disease that has no clear cause. Oorja Bio launched yesterday afternoon with $30 million and an in-licensed, novel approach to treat idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

news.title: Incyte pays Genesis $80M to expand AI-fueled drug discovery pact
news.summary: Incyte is paying Genesis $80 million upfront to expand use of its partner’s AI platform across a broader range of targets, continuing the biotech’s rapid embrace of AI in drug discovery and development.

news.title: BMS taps Anthropic’s Claude for enterprise-wide AI adoption to speed R&D, global workflows
news.summary: BMS is the latest large pharma company to make a major bet on AI, announcing a sweeping agreement with Anthropic to deploy its AI tool Claude as the “shared intelligence platform” across the drugmaker’s global operations.

news.title: Labcorp joins lab results personalization push with ‘MyLabcorp’ AI app
news.summary: The big new thing in medtech for 2026 is AI-powered apps and chatbots designed to help patients navigate their laboratory test results. Now, testing giant Labcorp is getting in on the act.

news.title: Immunovant pays out $39M to call time on FcRn inhibitor after eye disease phase 3 fails
news.summary: Immunovant has confirmed that its first-generation FcRn inhibitor has been scrapped, a month after the asset flunked a pair of phase 3 eye disease trials.

news.title: Endologix acquires thrombectomy system from Surmodics
news.summary: Endologix, a medtech that focuses on treatments for vascular diseases, expanded its offerings with the purchase of a mechanical thrombectomy system from Surmodics.

news.title: Parabilis, fresh from inking Regeneron pact, plans IPO to fund tumor drug’s phase 3 push
news.summary: With the ink barely dry on an antibody-drug conjugate-style deal with Regeneron, Parabilis Medicines has now turned its attention to the public markets.

news.title: Pfizer furthers pneumococcal efforts, remains ‘comfortable’ taking on emerging threats: vaccine chief
news.summary: After decades of developing vaccines to shield babies and adults from Streptococcus pneumonia infections, Pfizer is now taking another leap forward in its efforts to protect against one of the bacteria’s most dangerous strains—all while keeping an eye on the world’s several emerging global health threats.

news.title: 段永平重估英伟达
news.summary: 段永平两个季度23倍加仓英伟达，他看到了什么?

news.title: 2026CT行研报告：超高端CT引领、国产球管加速崛起
news.summary: 2025年CT行业量稳价升，国产替代加速，球管成投资热点

news.title: AI 看我上厕所，还要听我打呼噜
news.summary: 智能马桶、智能床，家居能靠AI翻红吗？

news.title: 36氪出海·活动｜“北京-卡塔尔”投资合作高峰洽谈会开启中
news.summary: 本次洽谈会由卡塔尔投资促进局（Invest Qatar）联合卡塔尔国民银行主办、北京市贸促会支持、36氪出海承办，欢迎报名。

papers.title: Conditional Equivalence of DPO and RLHF: Implicit Assumption, Failure Modes, and Provable Alignment
papers.summary: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) has emerged as a popular alternative to Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), offering theoretical equivalence with simpler implementation. We prove this equivalence is conditional rather than universal, depending on an implicit assumption frequently violated in practice: the RLHF-optimal policy must prefer human-preferred responses. When this assumption fails, DPO optimizes relative advantage over the reference policy rather than absolute alignment with human preferences, leading to pathological convergence where policies decrease DPO loss whi

papers.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
papers.summary: Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) show promise in medical applications, but their inability to faithfully ground responses in visual evidence raises serious concerns about clinical trustworthiness. While visual attribution methods are widely used to explain LVLM predictions, whether these explanations actually reflect the visual evidence underlying the model's decision is largely unverified, since ground-truth annotations for internal model reasoning are typically unavailable. We address this question for chest X-ray (CXR) reasoning by developing a causal evaluation framework that retains o

papers.title: PixVerve: Advancing Native UHR Image Generation to 100MP with a Large-Scale High-Quality Dataset
papers.summary: Text-to-Image (T2I) models have recently seen notable progress around 1K and 2K resolution. With the extreme desire for better visual experience and the rapid development of imaging technology, the demand for Ultra-High-Resolution (UHR) image generation has grown significantly. However, UHR image generation poses great challenges due to the scarcity and complexity of high-resolution content. In this paper, we first introduce PixVerve-95K, a high-quality, open-source UHR T2I dataset curated with a carefully designed data pipeline, which contains 95K images across diverse scenarios (each image h
